entries.summary: Student protesters demanded that Nobel laureate Yunus lead the new interim government.
Bangladesh's president office announced Yunus would lead the interim administration.
Al Jazeera feature mapped the big challenges ahead for the new interim leader.
Yunus headed home from Paris to assume the interim leadership role.
Yunus returned to Bangladesh ahead of his swearing-in ceremony.
Yunus took oath as chief adviser of the new interim government.
Yunus's government said it would seek Hasina's extradition from India.
Yunus said election roadmap would follow needed constitutional and institutional reforms.
Hasina's former ministers faced massacre charges as probe deadlines were announced.
Al Jazeera asked why Yunus was reportedly considering resigning from his role.
Leaked recording revealed Hasina had ordered firing on protesters during her rule.
Anniversary coverage examined the selling-the-revolution critique of Yunus government.
Al Jazeera feature questioned whether extrajudicial killings had continued under Yunus.
Awami League supporters protested across Bangladesh over crackdown and Hasina's trial.
International Crimes Tribunal under Yunus government convicted Hasina in absentia.
Al Jazeera analysed Bangladesh's post-conviction test under Yunus's interim leadership.
Killing of a 2024 uprising activist triggered fresh unrest under Yunus's watch.
Bangladesh's president office named Yunus to head the new interim government.
CNN profiled Yunus as the student protesters' chosen interim government leader.
Yunus was sworn in as chief adviser of Bangladesh's new interim government.
Students rallied at vandalised Hasina mural in early days of new government.
First murder case opened against the former prime minister under Yunus's government.
Yunus's inspection revealed secret Aynaghar detention centers used by previous government.
Bangladesh court under Yunus tenure issued an arrest warrant for Hasina.
Yunus indicated elections could be held by the end of 2025.
Thousands rallied at the March for Unity in Dhaka under interim government.
Activists demolished Hasina's father's historic Dhaka home during Yunus's tenure.
UN human rights office reported up to 1,400 killed during 2024 crackdown.
CNN examined the uncertain future of the Awami League under Yunus.
Bangladesh tribunal under Yunus sentenced Hasina to imprisonment in absentia.
Tribunal under Yunus formally indicted Hasina for the 2024 protester killings.
International Crimes Tribunal convicted Hasina in absentia during Yunus's tenure.
Corruption trial began involving Hasina's UK Labour MP niece Tulip Siddiq.
